name: Azure Blob Storage Python SDK
pricing_notes: Azure pricing: LRS ~$0.018/GB/mo; hot tier write $0.0065/10k ops

auth.notes: Connection string, account key, SAS token, or azure-identity credential

performance.notes: Multi-part upload for large blobs; parallel transfers via max_concurrency; 5TB max blob

agent_readiness.notes: Object storage for Azure Blob. from azure.storage.blob import BlobServiceClient. client = BlobServiceClient.from_connection_string(conn_str). blob = client.get_blob_client(container='mycontainer', blob='file.txt'). blob.upload_blob(data). blob.download_blob().readall(). List: client.get_container_client('c').list_blobs(). SAS URL generation. Event Grid events on upload/delete. Batch delete. Hot/Cool/Cold/Archive tiers. Data Lake Storage Gen2 (hierarchical namespace): DataLakeServiceClient. Async: from azure.storage.blob.aio import BlobServiceClient. Azurite emulator for local dev.

gotchas: Three separate storage SDKs: azure-storage-blob, azure-storage-queue, azure-storage-file-share
Blob metadata must be strings (no nested objects)
Container names must be lowercase (gotcha for migration from S3)
Azurite local emulator required for local testing (no free sandbox)
best_when: Data Lake Storage Gen2 for big data analytics (hierarchical namespace)
Event-driven pipelines on blob upload (Event Grid trigger)
Tiered storage: Hot → Cool → Archive for cost optimization
avoid_when: Multi-cloud storage (use S3-compatible API with boto3 or MinIO)
AWS workloads — boto3 S3 is equivalent but for AWS
summary: Azure Blob Storage Python SDK for cloud object storage. BlobServiceClient.from_connection_string(conn_str). blob.upload_blob(data), blob.download_blob().readall(). SAS URL generation, Event Grid triggers, Hot/Cool/Archive tiers. Data Lake Storage Gen2 via DataLakeServiceClient for ADLS hierarchical namespaces. Async support. Azurite emulator for local development. Three separate packages: azure-storage-blob, azure-storage-queue, azure-storage-file-share.
